title: carthamus hispanicus
intro: Carthamus hispanicus, commonly known as safflower, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the daisy family and is native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and reduce pain. The health benefits of Carthamus hispanicus include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting potential anticancer properties. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and capsules, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
